2002-03-01.title: World Briefing | Africa: Congo: Agreement Could Let Talks Resume
2002-03-01.abstract: Congo's two main rebel groups agree to let political parties select delegates to talks on country's political future (S)

2002-03-01.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: Gearing Up For Presidential Bid?
2002-03-01.abstract: South Korea's Park Geun Hye considers running in next presidential election; would be first woman to do so; photo (S)

2002-03-01.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: Clearing An I.M.F. Hurdle
2002-03-01.abstract: Argentina's 23 provincial governors sign accord with President Eduardo Duhalde requiring them to reduce budget deficits; accord paves way for austerity budget, International Monetary Fund condition for new aid (S)

2002-03-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: More Fallout From 1945
2002-03-01.abstract: German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder cancels visit to Prague, avoiding involvement in dispute between Czechs and Hungarians stemming from end of World War II over citizenship and land rights (S)

2002-03-01.title: Israeli Troops Raid Arab Camps, Killing 11 in West Bank Fighting
2002-03-01.abstract: Israeli troops for first time battle their way into two refugee camps in Jenin on West Bank, killing at least 11 Palestinians and losing one soldier in some of fiercest fighting of 17-month conflict; hospital officials say dozens of Palestinians are wounded; Israeli government says it wants to deny Palestinian militants havens to manufacture explosives and plan attacks; militants promise reprisals, particularly for attack on Balata Refugee Camp, stronghold of Yasir Arafat's Fatah faction; gunmen then open fire on Gilo, Jewish neighborhood of Jerusalem occupied in 1967 war; new violence comes as Palestinian officials and Arab leaders praise initiative floated by Saudi Arabia to resolve deepening impasse; photos (M)

2002-03-02.title: Reinventing grunge, Naples-style
2002-03-02.abstract: NAPLES—  Noodles and pizza have spread from Naples throughout the world. Could Neapolitan neo-grunge also be exported?

2002-03-02.title: U.S. Broadens Terror Fight, Readying Troops for Yemen
2002-03-02.abstract: Pres Bush has approved plans to send as many as 100 troops, mostly Special Forces, to train Yemeni military to fight terrorists, specifically Al Qaeda fighters hiding in country; increasingly broad campaign stirs Democratic complaints that administration demands virually unlimited funds without answering to Congress; latest operations aim to crush isolated but potent Islamic insurgents; Special Forces would be limited to training and equipping nations requesting help, but not calling in air strikes; Americans will be discouraged from even firing weapons except in self-defense; Yemen is key center of support for Osama bin Laden, home to well-armed tribal forces, at least 20 senior Qaeda officials and scores of fighters trained in Afghanistan (M)

2002-03-02.title: Experts Dispute Bush Aide's Criticism of War Crimes Panels
2002-03-02.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan and international law experts strongly defend UN war crimes tribunals for Balkans and Rwanda, in response to US envoy Pierre-Richard Prosper's testimony to Congress casting doubt on tribunals' integrity and professionalism; South Africa Justice Richard Goldstone, first chief prosecutor, criticizes Bush administration position that tribunals should wind up work by 2007-2008; experts concede management problems at Rwanda tribunal, but not at Balkan proceedings in The Hague (M)

2002-03-02.title: Cuba Arrests Asylum Seekers in Mexican Embassy
2002-03-02.abstract: Cuban police arrest 21 asylum seekers holed up inside Mexican Embassy after crashing bus through gates; Mexican officials request raid after concluding young men are economic, not political, refugees (S)

2002-03-02.title: For glamorous dressing, the city's nightclubs steal the show : In London, forget the catwalks
2002-03-02.abstract: LONDON—  London has come to a pretty pass when the parties upstage the catwalk shows. So frantic was the social whirl surrounding London Fashion Week that many a fashion editor bypassed the catwalk shows altogether.

2002-03-03.title: Previous U.S. Deaths
2002-03-03.abstract: Pentagon announces death of American soldier in Afghanistan, bringing number of American service members killed by hostile fire while engaged in war on terrorism to two (S)

2002-03-03.title: Israelis Say Arms Are Found In Search of 2 Refugee Camps
2002-03-03.abstract: Israeli army says its soldiers have found six makeshift rockets and stores of bomb-making chemicals at refugee camps in Balata and Jenin on West Bank; soldiers continue to occupy many cinder-black homes on third day of two raids that Israel says has left 30 Palestinians dead; Palestinians in both camps accuse soldiers of firing at random, killing several civilians and terrifying residents; most Palestinian gunmen have deserted both camps but gunfire still rings out periodically (M)

2002-03-03.title: China Punishes a Christian Home for the Aged
2002-03-03.abstract: Officials in suburb of Beijing cut off electricity for small Christian-run home for aged and threaten to shut off water supply unless home is disbanded; they are alarmed by unauthorized church services there; religious activities outside China's official church are illegal, but so called house church services such as ones in home for aged are widespread, sometimes tolerated and sometimes not (M)

2002-03-03.title: Trial Centers on Role of Press During Rwanda Massacre
2002-03-03.abstract: Three former Rwandan news media executives stand accused of genocide and incitement to genocide through their use of radio broadcasts and newspapers; their trial before United Nations war crimes tribunal will examine scope of role played by news media in massacre of more than 800,000 people in Rwanda in 1994; this is first time group of journalists stand accused before international tribunal on such grave charges since 1946, when Julius Streicher, Nazi publisher of anti-Semitic weekly Der Sturmer, appeared before Nuremberg judges; prosecutors in Rwanda case are drawing stark parallels between Der Sturmer's vitriolic campaigns against Jews before World War II and actions of some Rwandan media before and during 1994 slaughter of Tutsi; legal specialists believe outcome of current trial may set crucial precedent for future international cases; photos (M)

2002-03-03.title: U.S. Seeks DNA Of All Captives In Afghan War
2002-03-03.abstract: Federal authorities want to create DNA databank of terrorism suspects by analyzing blood samples from thousands of captured fighters of Al Qaeda and Taliban being held in Afghanistan and at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba; FBI authorities say they are frustrated by their inability to identify vast majority of detainees, and worried that many of them will be released before government is certain who they are; say information from proposed DNA database could also play role in some current investigations, including case of Richard Reid, accused of trying to blow up trans-Atlantic flight with explosives hidden in his shoes; say DNA might help them trace source of hairs found in Reid's shoes and possibly identify accomplice; DNA database proposal is being reviewed by Justice Dept; Congressional approval would be required; civil liberties groups are likely to fight such attempts to expand use of DNA profiling (M)

2002-03-04.title: Fear Stalls Health Push In India
2002-03-04.abstract: Mistaken belief that dozens of children died from doses of vitamin A halts public health drive in Assam, India, that international experts say is essential to save tens of thousands of children from dying of variety of diseases; decision by national government to halt campaign underscores politics and suspicion that can surround even best-intended efforts at foreign assistance in India; even though public health officials say none of deaths was caused by vitamin A, government has turned matter over to committee of experts who will recommend strategy in coming weeks, which will include decision on whether campaign should be restarted or ended for good; photos; map (M)

2002-03-04.title: Frozen by Violence: An American Mission Waits for Its Opening
2002-03-04.abstract: News analysis of outlook for US diplomacy in Middle East as Israeli-Palestinian violence intensifies; unrelenting violence seems all but certain to bolster Bush administration's position that meaningful engagement will have to wait; has left Bush administration stymied and efforts at meaningful diplomacy stalled (M)

2002-03-04.title: For Israelis, New Tragedy Is a Challenge Sent by God
2002-03-04.abstract: Suicide bombing in Orthodox section of East Jerusalem that left nine Israelis dead is seen by many yeshiva students as painful reprimand from God and as part of chain of violence dating back to biblical times (M)

2002-03-04.title: High speed, high society : Formula One swerves through a downturn
2002-03-04.abstract: The Formula One auto-racing circuit is a high-speed crossroads of death and danger, big bucks and big stakes, where leaders of nations and big business hobnob with, promote and cheer for young men whose only skill is driving very fast machines that look more like rockets than cars.

2002-03-04.title: U.S. and Philippine Governments Revive Old Relationship
2002-03-04.abstract: Special relationship forged between US and Philippines during World War II, which soured in early 1990's, is revived in aftermath of September 11 terrorist attacks; most visible sign of revival is arrival of American troops to work with Filipino soldiers to defeat Abu Sayyaf terrorists holding two Americans hostage; two nations are discussing treaty that would give US right to store weapons, permanent overflight rights for combat aircraft and right to set up camps in archipelago for short periods; photo (M)

2002-03-05.title: World Briefing | Americas: Haiti: New Prime Minister
2002-03-05.abstract: Haitian Pres Jean-Bertrand Aristide selects Senator Yvon Neptune as prime minister to replace Jean-Marie Cherestal, who resigned amid complaints of corruption; photo (S)

2002-03-05.title: World Briefing | Asia: Thailand: Under Pressure, Magazine Apologizes
2002-03-05.abstract: Far Eastern Economic Review apologizes for recent article about Thailand's government and monarchy; police weigh lifting expulsion orders for two of magazine's reporters (S)

2002-03-05.title: 7 AMERICANS DIE DURING RAID ON AFGHANISTAN MOUNTAINS
2002-03-05.abstract: Seven American soldiers are killed in some of fiercest fighting of five-month war in Afghanistan as US ground forces take lead in large-scale combat operation for first time; die in incidents involving two helicopters, one hit by rocket-propelled grenade, while attempting to drop off reconaissance teams during heavy fighting in nearly inaccessible mountains near Gardez in east; deaths, along with that of Special Forces soldier day earlier, bring total casualties to eight killed and approximately 40 wounded since start of operation on March 2 to wipe out pocket of hard-core Al Qaeda fighters; operation involving approximately 2000 allied troops described; commander Gen Tommy R Franks comments; Pres Bush voices sadness over loss of American servicemen, but says he is determined to fulfill mission troops were sent to perform and to make sure US is free from further attack; photos; map; diagram (M)

2002-03-05.title: U.S. Moves to Close Canadian Drug Route for Illegal Stimulant
2002-03-05.abstract: American and Canadian law enforcement officials say illegal production in US of popular stimulants like methamphetamine reflects lax regulations in Canada for chemical ingredients; say Canada has become as result leading supply route for raw ingredients to US where substances are more tightly controlled; US Customs Service has seized in last 11 months more than 110 million tablets of decongestants that contain primary ingredients for making methamphetamine or 'speed' (M)

2002-03-05.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: More Trouble At Foreign Ministry
2002-03-05.abstract: Japanese Foreign Ministry admits Parliament member Muneo Suzuki exerted improper influence over ministry's policies and bidding for government contracts (S)

2002-03-06.title: After Deadly Firestorm, India Officials Ask Why
2002-03-06.abstract: Indian probers seek to learn what spurred Muslim mob to set fire to train loaded with Hindu activists, Godhra, burning alive 58 people, mostly women and children, and sparking reprisal attacks on Muslims across Gujarat state; Godhra remains under tight curfew; city's Muslim mayor, two Muslim city council members and 38 Muslims who live along railway tracks have been arrested; police in Ahmedabad reportedly charge local Hindu politicians and World Hindu Council leaders encouraged reprisals; Godhra has history of Muslim-Hindu violence and open mutual contempt and mistrust; map; photo (M)

2002-03-06.title: U.S. cool to report of Israeli spy ring
2002-03-06.abstract: WASHINGTON:—  American officials expressed caution and doubts Tuesday about reports that a large ring of Israelis spies had been broken up last year in the United States, where some of them allegedly had come in pursuit of Al Qaeda militants.

2002-03-06.title: Mubarak Urges U.S. Role in Stopping Mideast Bloodshed
2002-03-06.abstract: Egyptian Pres Hosni Mubarak urges United States to intervene to stop mounting bloodshed between Israelis and Palestinians, speech, Washington; Pres Bush gives no sign that US will step up its role; says he and Mubarak view situation 'with great alarm,' joint news conference; calls Saudi Crown Prince Abdullah's peace proposal positive development; lauds Mubarak's offer to broker meeting between Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat and Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon; photo (M)

2002-03-06.title: Chinese Hear From Premier On Threats To Economy
2002-03-06.abstract: Chinese Prime Min Zhu Rongji calls for renewed assault on official corruption and for bolstering farm incomes, televised State of Nation speech opening annual Parliament session; highlights threats to prosperity and social stability he says dog transition to market economy; hails 2001 achievements, including 7.3 percent economic growth rate (M)

2002-03-06.title: Zanzibar Journal; Tourists and Islam Mingle, Not Always Cozily
2002-03-06.abstract: Conflict in Zanzibar, Tanzania, spurred by dual roles as Islamic society and tourist spot has intensified as segment of its Muslim community pushes hardline religious view; anti-alcohol teachings in local mosques have spurred series of bombings at local bars, none regular tourist haunts, tarnishing island's image as tranquil resort; map; photos (M)

2002-03-07.title: World Briefing | Europe: Belgium: Date Set For Sharon Hearing
2002-03-07.abstract: Belgian court sets May 15 for hearing on whether case can proceed against Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon for 1982 massacre of Palestinians in two Lebanese refugee camps (S)

2002-03-07.title: U.S. ADDS TROOPS AND HELICOPTERS IN AFGHAN BATTLE
2002-03-07.abstract: Commanders call in more American troops and helicopters to counter attempts by Taliban and Al Qaeda to bolster Islamic fighters struggling to hold on to their mountaintop redoubts; 300 more troops are called to battle, for total of 1,200 American combat soldiers; 21 more helicopters are also added; American and Afghan commanders say up to 500 new fighters have joined Taliban and Qaeda force of 200 men; there is growing sense that battle could be climactic moment of conflict that may decide whether Taliban and Al Qaeda meet their end as fighting force in Afghanistan; American commanders say US-led bombing attacks and ground assaults may have already killed as many as 400 hostile fighters of total of 800; photos; map; chart (L)

2002-03-07.title: Bush Officials End Support Of Sharon's Tough Stance
2002-03-07.abstract: Bush administration decides to terminate its unequivocal support for Prime Min Ariel Sharon's hard-line strategy and declares that his approach will likely fail; administration officials are alarmed by Sharon's recent comment that Palestinians must be 'hit hard' because they will only negotiate after they are beaten; officials decide to respond strongly to Sharon, lest he interpret Washington's silence as implicit permission to intensify conflict or declare all-out war; Secretary of State Colin Powell's comments to House subcommittee asking Israel to take 'hard look' at its policies are sharpest criticism to date of Sharon's tough policy; Sharon quickly responds that he, like Pres Bush, is simply fighting terrorism (M)

2002-03-07.title: Ahmedabad Journal; In India, a Child's Life Is Cheap Indeed
2002-03-07.abstract: Twenty-one Muslim boys and girls burned to death by raging Hindu mobs and unclaimed in city mortuary--perhaps because all relatives died also--are buried in Ahmedabad in Gujarat state; Muslim disillusionment is heightened by government decision on compensation to families; families of Hindu victims of train attack by Muslims will receive twice as much as families of Muslim victims of Hindu attacks in reprisal; government calls train attack terrorism, and later rioting Hindu-Muslim violence; Muslims are angry at police force that failed to protect them and at state chief minister who blamed Muslims for provoking mob violence directed at them; photo; map (M)

2002-03-07.title: Rumsfeld's Burden: Stilling Echoes of the Grisly Raid in Somalia
2002-03-07.abstract: Analysis: Defense Sec Donald H Rumseld seeks to differentiate between disastrous American raid in Mogadishu, Somalia, in 1993 that led to deaths of 18 American soldiers, and recent military events in Afghanistan, when Navy Seal fell out of helicopter and died, perhaps at hands of enemy, and six commandos were killed and dozen wounded before his body was retrieved; there are similarities, including significant underestimation of enemy's will to mobilize and fight; but contrasts are more striking; this time Pentagon has clear purpose: destruction of Al Qaeda and Taliban government; it also has broad support of American public; still, Pentagon's willingness to accept casualties is far from clear (M)

2002-03-08.title: Coat, Backpack, Sweat: Close Call in Israeli Cafe
2002-03-08.abstract: Man enters restaurant in Jerusalem wearing heavy jacket on warm, sunny afternoon, attracting attention of owner, waiter and security guard; man is found to be wearing bomb with detonator switch in his hand; incident leaves restaurant workers and patrons jumpy but grateful; photo (M)

2002-03-08.title: Zimbabwe President's Rival Vows to Prevail at the Polls
2002-03-08.abstract: Morgan Tsvangirai, opposition presidential candidate in Zimbabwe, says he hopes to be alive to play role in government; has faced three assassination attempts since standing up to government five years ago as leader of country's largest trade union; one of Pres Robert Mugabe's aides said publicly that week that he would support military coup if Tsvangirai wins election; thousands of people have put their hopes in him; he is first politician to build formidable coalition of blacks and whites powerful enough to challenge Mugabe; photo (M)

2002-03-08.title: Scientists Discover a Dinosaur Clad in Flightless Feathers
2002-03-08.abstract: Chinese and American paleontologists find fossils of feathered, flightless dinosaur, slightly bigger than pheasant, that they say should settle once and for all that feathers originated before birds or flight; discovery of 125-million-year-old dinosaur in China is reported in journal Nature; scientists suspect feathers evolved for some purpose other than flight, perhaps for warmth, and only later and with other physical changes were they used to help takeoffs (M)

2002-03-08.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: A Warning From China
2002-03-08.abstract: Japan weighs whether to salvage ship that sank in gun battle in East China Sea (S)

2002-03-08.title: The Collections / Paris : Dior's magpie magnificence
2002-03-08.abstract: PARIS—  With skirts whirling, fur from Mohican boots flying and colors vibrating along with the 1980s dance music, John Galliano finally got Dior products out on the runway. His joyous show brought pep and energy to the opening day of the Paris autumn/winter 2002 shows Thursday and proved why Dior's latest figures are as hot as its designer's clothes.

2002-03-09.title: Corner of England in Paris gets a colorful makeover
2002-03-09.abstract: PARIS—  With a burst of color, one of the city's oldest shopping destinations, Old England, has been recently redone, turning it into one of Paris's most modern shopping venues.

2002-03-09.title: The Collections / Paris : Forward-looking designers go back to their roots
2002-03-09.abstract: PARIS—  The powerful shows that have hit the runways — Bang! Bang! Bang! — in the first two days of the autumn/winter 2002 have a collective thread. Designers are going back to their roots.

2002-03-09.title: World Briefing | Europe: Macedonia: Amnesty For Rebels Approved
2002-03-09.abstract: Lawmakers in Macedonia pass amnesty for ethnic Albanian guerrillas, in move hailed by both sides as vital step to solidify tenuous peace; law does not cover possible crimes investigated by international tribunal in The Hague, which is said to be looking into incidents involving both Macedonians and Albanians (S)

2002-03-09.title: Pakistan Prepares to Expel Foreign Religious Students
2002-03-09.abstract: Senior Pakistani officials say government will expel thousands of Arab, Afghan and other foreign students now studying at religious schools across country, out of concern that their presence might be step toward possible militant activities; plan would also bar new enrollment of foreign students altogether; plan stems from promise made by Gen Pervez Musharraf in Jan to deport foreign students at madrassas who lacked valid visas; in past, Pakistan's government allowed madrassas to operate virtually unregulated; madrassas served as principal base for Islamic scholars who became leaders of Taliban movement in Afghanistan and fighters against American-led forces there; government also intends to release at least 800 of 2,000 Pakistani militants arrested in Jan as part of crackdown against extremist Muslim groups, provided they vow to abstain from violence and outlawed groups (M)

2002-03-09.title: Zimbabwe Vote Heralds a Shift in Southern Africa
2002-03-09.abstract: African diplomats converge on Harare to observe Zimbabwe's presidential election; Pres Robert Mugabe, fiery nationalist who nurtured and inspired generation of black leaders, is now old man with tarnished image, his popularity waning and his power seemingly slipping through his fingers; whether or not he wins re-election, emergence of formidable black opposition party in Zimbawe is already sending jitters all across southern Africa, where leaders are wrestling with similar strains: disaffected young people, voter apathy and mounting complaints about unemployment, poverty and empty stomachs; opposition party leaders in Zimbabwe say leaders of neighboring countries fear political challenges at home, and so are reluctant to condemn Zimbabwe's government attacks on opposition; photos (M)

2002-03-10.title: Drug Kingpin, Long Sought, Is Captured By Mexicans
2002-03-10.abstract: Mexican authorities announce arrest of Benjamin Arellano Felix, chief of nation's most powerful and violent drug gang; Asa Hutchinson, head of United States Drug Enforcement Administration, hails arrest as 'incredible victory'; Mexican authorities say that under interrogation, Arellano Felix confirmed death of his brother Ramon, cartel's notorious enforcer; say death of one brother and capture of another represent one of biggest breakthroughs for law enforcement in Mexico's long history of drug wars (M)

2002-03-10.title: Sudan's War Bars Cure For Disease Eradicated In Other Parts of Africa
2002-03-10.abstract: International Conference on Eradication of Dracunculiasis, commonly known as Guinea worm disease, draws hundreds of scientists, politicians and others to Khartoum, Sudan; Guinea worm disese occurs in just 13 countries around world and could be eradicated altogether if it were not for Sudan, country with more cases than any other; health experts note that Sudan has 80 percent of world's reported Guinea worm cases, virtually all of them in war-ravaged south where clinics are rarity and aerial bombardments by government troops are commonplace; 18-year-long war in Sudan has made impossible inoculations, treatments and health education campaigns that are imperative in controlling any disease; photo; map showing cases of Guinea worm disease worldwide (M)

2002-03-10.title: 14 Israelis Killed in Two Terror Attacks
2002-03-10.abstract: Palestinian terror attacks in Jerusalem and in Netanya kill at least 14 Israelis, underscoring enormous challenges facing America's renewed peacemaking efforts; in Jerusalem, suicide bomber walks into popular cafe and sets off bomb, killing 11 people and wounding 50 others; militant Islamic group Hamas claims responsibility and says bomber lived in refugee camp in Hebron; in Netanya, two men attack popular promenade, hurling grenade into hotel lobby and opening fire with automatic rifles until police officers shoot them dead; three people are killed, including baby and Israeli-Arab man, and 24 others are injured; Aksa Martyrs Brigades, which is associated with Yasir Arafat's Fatah organization, claims responsibility for attack and says attackers came from refugee camp in Nablus; Israeli helicopters fire missiles at Arafat's seaside office building in Gaza City, detroying it; photos; maps showing locations of Israeli and Palestinian deaths in last week (L)

2002-03-10.title: Slim Edge Mars Vote by Swiss To Join U.N.
2002-03-10.abstract: Switzerland votes to join United Nations, but narrow margin of victory is expected to hobble any more such overtures for near future; exceptionally high voter turnout underlines divisions between urban centers that favor membership and rural heartland areas that are opposed (S)

2002-03-10.title: Esther Ocloo, 83, Pioneer in Microloans to Help Women Become Entrepreneurs, Dies
2002-03-10.abstract: Esther Afua Ocloo, who pioneered small loans for women, dies at age 83; photo (M)

2002-03-11.title: Trendy and sophisticated styles replace boring beige bras and panties : Asia brightens up its lingerie, discreetly
2002-03-11.abstract: HONG KONG—  Why spend money on nice underwear, when nobody sees it? That used to be the reasoning of Asian consumers who went in for utilitarian styles in basic colors. But today women are discarding their boring beige bras and panties and donning more feminine lace-embellished styles in pretty pastel colors.

2002-03-11.title: Saying Battle Is Reaching End, U.S. Sends Troops Back to Base
2002-03-11.abstract: American commanders, while not declaring victory, cautiously declare crux of battle against Al Qaeda ad Taliban fighters in much of Shah-i-Kot Valley in Afghanistan effectively is over; order about 400 soldiers back to base but say say fresh troops may be rotated into battle to eliminate remnants of enemy; Joint Chiefs of Staff Chmn Gen Richard B Myers says remaining Al Qaeda and Taliban fighters are widely dispersed; hints battle has opened way to eventual end of American combat operations in Afghanistan; jubilant returning soldiers from 10th Mountain Division in Fort Drum, NY, express confidence battle will mark decisive moment in successful war; maps; photos (M)

2002-03-11.title: Taliban and War Deliver a Double Blow to Villagers
2002-03-11.abstract: Shah-i-Kot, collection of mountain villages in eastern Afghanistan that is scene of fierce fighting between US-led forces and those of Al Qaeda and Taliban, is dealt double blow; residents were forced to leave by Taliban who favored it as place to make last stand; was home to mujahedeen base in 1980's during war against Russians, and is considered hallowed ground; is currently being destroyed by bombs dropped by US warplanes; photo (M)

2002-03-11.title: Architects fear boutique craze is going too far
2002-03-11.abstract: WASHINGTON—  Fashion's flirtation with architecture has led to a new generation of over-the-top boutiques. But the design elite is still struggling to embrace the shop-til-you-drop movement.

2002-03-11.title: U.S. Tries To Explain New Policy For A-Bomb
2002-03-11.abstract: Sec of State Colin L Powell says there is no cause for international alarm over secret Pentagon policy review that identifies countries like Iran, Iraq and North Korea as potential targets for future American nuclear attacks; Powell's remarks are echoed by Joint Chiefs of Staff chairman Gen Richard B Myers and other top foreign policy and national security aides to help calm fears among European groups and Middle Eastern leaders that disclosure of administration's ambitious nuclear plans suggest that American nuclear attack is imminent as part of war on terrorism; photos (M)

2002-03-12.title: Official Arrested as Zimbabwe Election Ends
2002-03-12.abstract: Police in Harare, Zimbabwe, arrest Welshman Ncube, an opposition leader, and detain four American diplomats observing presidential election, which ended after court denied opposition request to extend vote after third day of sometimes chaotic balloting; developments draw strong protest from US State Department, which calls it 'totally unacceptable;' photo (M)

2002-03-12.title: In London, putting touch of whimsy into jewelry
2002-03-12.abstract: LONDON—  I wanted to created something that was pure, yet totally luxurious," says the young British designer Russell Sage of the transparent twists of glass jewelry he created for his recent autumn 2002 London show. Known for using delicate fabrics such as antique French and Gainsborough silks, this season Sage is concerned with the more exclusive aspects of fashion. "Now it's time to concentrate on luxury and quality," he says.

2002-03-12.title: India Seeks to Head Off Any New Violence
2002-03-12.abstract: India's Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee, facing anger over failure of governing party in Gujarat State to prevent killing of hundreds of Muslims by Hindu mobs, assures Parliament he will keep peace in another potential flash point: northern Indian city of Ayodyha; militant Hindus who belong to group affiliated with Vajpayee's Hindu nationalist Bhatariya Janata Party say they will hold ceremony in Ayodhya on March 15 as part of campaign to build temple on site of 16th-century mosque they helped tear down nine years ago; photo (M)

2002-03-12.title: With Relief and Sarcasm, Soldiers Recall Whizzing Bullets Fired by 'Wimps'
2002-03-12.abstract: American soldiers returning from combat against Al Qaeda and Taliban fighters in Shah-i-Kot mountains of Afghanistan give first-hand accounts of battle; say enemy appears vanquished, but is willing to fight to death; photo (M)

2002-03-12.title: A Village at Source of Heroin Trade Fears the Eradication of Its Poppies
2002-03-12.abstract: New Afghan government risks sanctions from donor nations, especially US, since any aid may depend on destruction of poppy fields, which are sources of most of heroin found on streets of Europe and America; government, however, will also face opposition from farmers who depend on poppy cultivation for living; plight of poppy farmers in Sangin in Helmand Province, where opium markets thrived under Taliban but are now outlawed, and where fields may be eradicated discussed; maps; photos (M)

2002-03-13.title: U.N. Shivers In a Season Of Cost Cuts That Pinch
2002-03-13.abstract: General Assembly has slashed operating budget of United Nations by $75 million over two years; thermostats are lowered to save heating costs, leaving many meeting rooms too cold to sit in without coat and gloves (M)

2002-03-13.title: U.N. Chief Tells Israel It Must End 'Illegal Occupation'
2002-03-13.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan tells Israel to end 'illegal occupation' of Palestinian lands, as Israeli forces carry out fiercest assault since areas were captured in 1967 war; 31 Palestinians are killed as troops pursue armed Palestinians and round up young men in hunt for terrorists; photos; map; six Israelis are killed when gunmen disguised as Israeli soldiers open fire on bus and cars near Lebanon border; violence comes as Vice Pres Cheney begins tour of 11 nations to rally Arab support against Iraq, and further undermines prospects for Gen Anthony C Zinni's cease-fire mission; Israeli tanks enter Ramallah amid boom of Palestinian grenades and clatter of Israeli machine guns; Annan statement condemns Palestinian terrorist attacks on innocent civilians as 'morally repugnant'; says Israel has right to live in peace within secure recognized borders, but must end occupation, bombings, unnecessary lethal force and daily humiliations of ordinary Palestinians; tells Palestinians they have right to state but must stop terrorism that makes Israelis believe their very existence, not occupation, is target; Israeli official likens Israeli raids to US attack in Afghanistan, which Annan supported as self-defense (M)

2002-03-13.title: Cheney, in Jordan, Meets Opposition to Military Move in Iraq
2002-03-13.abstract: Vice Pres Dick Cheney is in Amman, beginning most difficult phase of his 12-day mission to elicit allied support for possible military campaign against Iraq; he encounters immediate opposition from King Abdullah II; Jordanian officials issue statement saying king is urging that Bush administration dispute with Iraq be resolved through dialogue and peaceful means; strong comments from king's officials, and decision to make them public, reflect concern that American offensive against Iraq would open another front in region already racked by instability; Jordan's large Palestinian population and extensive trade with Iraq have long fed Jordanian reluctance to support Washington's stance toward Baghdad; photo (M)

2002-03-13.title: Roy Porter, 55, Historian, 'A One-Man Book Factory'
2002-03-13.abstract: Dr Roy Porter, British author, commentator and historian, dies at age 55 (M)

2002-03-13.title: Taipei Journal; In Taiwan, a Scandal a Day Keeps the Blues Away
2002-03-13.abstract: Taiwan has weathered lurid and tacky scandals in recent months; pirated copies of video depicting single female politician in tryst with married lover flooded island in Dec; since then, Taiwan's news media has ladled out almost-daily dollops of love and lust among politicians; this is far cry from two years ago, when newspapers were full of thoughtful analysis about relations with China; photo (M)

2002-03-14.title: Marooned Taliban Count Out Grim Hours in an Afghan Jail
2002-03-14.abstract: About 3,000 Taliban prisoners, captured in Kunduz, have been living in miserable conditions in Afghan jail for last four months; Americans interviewed them and took worst of lot to Gunatanamo naval base in Cuba; Uzbek government took about 10 of its citizens; warden freed about 250, most of them either very old or very sick, before recent Muslim holiday; remaining prisoners, all of them Afghan or Pakistani, appear to be largely forgotten; 800 Pakistanis face especially uncertain future; 3,000 men are living in 40 cells in prison, in Shibarghan, Afghanistan, designed to hold about 800; there is little food and hardly any medical aid or medicine; exactly how many prisoners have died remains mystery; photo (M)

2002-03-14.title: Fatal Attack Shatters Israeli Border Town's Calm
2002-03-14.abstract: Relative peace in town of Shlomi, Israel, on Lebanon border, has been shattered by two Arab gunmen who kill six Israelis near town; it is most serious attack in area since Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon in May 2000; attack is claimed by Al Aksa Martyrs Brigades, linked to mainstream Palestinian group Al Fatah; Israeli military officials say it could have been work of Hezbollah; photos (M)

2002-03-14.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Migrants Stifle Channel Traffic
2002-03-14.abstract: Hundreds of immigrants try to board trains to go from France to England but are stymied by increased security at Channel Tunnel; virtually all freight service to Britain through tunnel is suspended (S)

2002-03-14.title: Taliban and Qaeda Death Toll In Mountain Battle Is a Mystery
2002-03-14.abstract: American military officials acknowledge that Taliban and Al Qaeda death tolls they announce nearly every day are just estimates; say that listing Qaeda fighter as 'confirmed dead' does not necessarily mean that military has a body; still, estimates exist, despite assertions of Pentagon officials that they are not in body-count business; photo (M)

2002-03-14.title: Tokyo Journal; 100,000 People Perished, but Who Remembers?
2002-03-14.abstract: American firebombing of Tokyo during World War II, which killed estimated 100,000 people and devastated 16 square miles of city, has left surprisingly few traces in popular memory of Japanese, or Americans; it occurred 57 years ago this week, in one horrific night, when 1,665 tons of napalm-filled bombs were dropped; apart from atomic bomb victims, little is done to honor Japan's civilian dead, partly because this might raise questions about Japanese leaders druing war and partly because of avid pursuit of friendship with America; one survivor, Hiroshi Hoshino, is banding together with other survivors to devote himself to preserving memory of people killed in Mar 10, 1954 bombings; photos (M)

2002-03-15.title: Zimbabwe Moderate Offers Assurances to Opposition
2002-03-15.abstract: John Nkomo, home affairs minister in Zimbabwe, acknowledges that next few months will be challenging; United States and Britain have assailed re-election of Pres Robert Mugabe as illegitimate and threaten to toughen sanctions against government's top leaders; western officials, who have already cut off most aid to Zimbabwe, vow to keep their pocketbooks shut; in past decade, poverty rate has climbed from 40 to 75 percent; photo (M)

2002-03-15.title: On Both Sides in the Mideast, Fear and Stress Are Building
2002-03-15.abstract: Fear and stress on both sides in Mideast is being felt even by household pets; Jerusalem veterinarian has taken to prescribing Valium for pet dogs so nervous from days and nights pierced by gunfire, explosions and sirens that they shake and will not eat; psychic shakiness of some pets is sign of how fear, and coping with it, has become stitched into daily life; in Ramallah, families sleep in basements, order their children not to go outside, and stretch out groceries to last until crossfire dies away; people on both sides are sick of living in fear, and that is chief resource for Anthony C Zinni, Bush administration envoy who arrived in hopes of arranging cease-fire; but along with fear has come anger, and that is Zinni's foremost obstacle; photos (M)

2002-03-15.title: Leonid Ozernoy, 62, Dissident And Soviet-Era Astrophysicist
2002-03-15.abstract: Dr Leonid M Ozernoy, Soviet-born astrophysicist and dissident whose campaign to emigrate to United States in 1980's drew international attention, dies at age 62 (M)

2002-03-15.title: U.S. SAYS POWELL DEMANDED PULLOUT BY ISRAELI FORCES
2002-03-15.abstract: Bush administration officials say Secretary of State Colin L Powell delivered blunt private demand to Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel on Mar 12 to withdraw from Palestinian-controlled areas to help American efforts to broker cease-fire, and that Amb Daniel C Kurtzer followed up with oral message to Sharon on following day, before American envoy Anthony C Zinni arrived in Jerusalem; Sharon has announced that army will begin staged withdrawal from Ramallah, but State Dept spokesman responds by saying US expects 'complete withdrawal' from all Palestinian-controlled areas; these demands, plus Pres Bush's stern reprimand to Israel on Mar 13, mark distinct policy shift after months of giving Israel virtual green light to move against Palestinians while blaming only Yasir Arafat for violence; Israelis say Sharon wants to avoid any confrontation with Washington, especially given rifts in his own government; photo; map (M)

2002-03-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Blood Victims Seek More Compensation
2002-03-15.abstract: Hemophiliacs infected with virus that causes AIDS by blood products provided by Irish state-run health services in 1980's in begin legal case to overturn 1991 compensation package and get more reparations (S)

2002-03-16.title: World Briefing | United Nations: U.S. May Regain Human Rights Seat
2002-03-16.abstract: US is likely to regain seat on UN Human Rights Commission, now that Italy and Spain reportedly agree to step aside (S)

2002-03-16.title: TREASURY'S CHIEF IS SAID TO FAULT STEEL TARIFF MOVE
2002-03-16.abstract: Treasury Sec Paul H O'Neill, in off-the-record comment following speech before Council on Foreign Relations in New York, says that he opposes Bush administration's recent decision to impose tariffs on imported steel and that move will cost more jobs in US than it will save; reiterates long-held position that imposing tariffs risks nation's interests as world's leader in promoting free trade (M)

2002-03-16.title: A corruption scandal looms over global tournament : Soccer chiefs play political game
2002-03-16.abstract: The World Cup of soccer kicks off in South Korea and Japan less than 80 days from now. But instead of keeping their eyes on the ball, instead of harmonizing efforts to manage and secure the games, soccer's overlords are betraying the sport they should serve.

2002-03-16.title: Cheney Says Next Goal In U.S. War on Terror Is to Block Access to Arms
2002-03-16.abstract: Vice Pres Cheney signals that Bush administration is determined to confront Iraq despite Arab objections, and says US goal after Afghanistan is to stop terrorism and prevent rogue states from developing weapons of mass destruction; speaks aboard aircraft carrier John C Stennis in Arabian Sea; photo (M)

2002-03-16.title: In Serbia, Politics in Turmoil As U.S. Diplomat Is Detained
2002-03-16.abstract: Serbian politics twist into chaos as US diplomat, John David Neighbor, is arrested and charged with espionage, along with senior Serb official, Momcilo Perisic; Neighbor is seized in restaurant and driven away with bag over his head, and held incommunicado for 15 hours; US expresses outrage (S)

2002-03-17.title: Coalition Widens Its Ocean Manhunt
2002-03-17.abstract: United States and its allies intensify their search for Taliban and Qaeda leaders in waters off Pakistan and Iran, convinced that American-led offensive in eastern Afghanistan has eliminated major terrorist haven and forced Taliban and Qaeda commanders to flee (M)

2002-03-17.title: Yugoslav Chief Defends Arrest Of American For Espionage
2002-03-17.abstract: Yugoslav Pres Vojislav Kostunica defends arrests of American diplomat John David Neighbor and senior Serbian government official Momcilo Perisic for espionage; local news media report men are suspected of leaking documents that could help convict former leader Slobodan Milosevic in his trial for war crimes at The Hague; Kostunica's statement will likely deepen his rift with Prime Min Zoran Djindjic, who has called arrests 'scandal'; Perisic has been released and Neighbor, who has diplomatic immunity, was released earlier; officials at American Embassy are outraged at arrest of Neighbor (M)

2002-03-17.title: U.N. to Open Rights Review With U.S. On Sidelines
2002-03-17.abstract: United Nations Human Rights Commission is set to begin annual review of abuses worldwide amid concerns that international commitment to human rights is being eroded by war against terrorism; human rights campaigners have accused United States, Russia, China and other countries of adopting security measures that jeopardize basic rights (M)

2002-03-17.title: Sudanese Foes Give Consent For Relief Aid For Civilians
2002-03-17.abstract: International effort led by United States to expand area where aid workers can operate safety in Sudan's civil war, one of Africa's longest, bears fruit with word that aid may soon be flowing to thousands of civilians isolated in rugged mountain region (M)

2002-03-17.title: Pakistan Seethes as the Militants Lash Out
2002-03-17.abstract: Doctors who are members of Pakistan's Shiite religious minority are targets of violence, victims of sectarian violence that has surged in Pakistan in last six weeks; attackers' real target appears to be government of Gen Pervez Musharraf; they are angry about his decision to side with United States in war against Taliban and his domestic policy of getting tough on terrorists at home; photo (M)

2002-03-18.title: Rebels or Drug Rings Suspected in Colombian Prelate's Killing
2002-03-18.abstract: Assassination of Archbishop Isaias Duarte Cancino of Cali, Colombia, after officiating over group wedding in poor neighborhood, stuns country already deep in conflict; Colombian law enforcement officials hint rebels are most likely responsible, while church officials say narcotics traffickers may have killed him; his excommunication of rebels for kidnapping civilians and his castigation of drug-tainted candidates in recent congressional elections noted; his involvement in country's civil war and his help in arranging secret meetings between government officials and outlawed rightist paramilitary group responsible for thousands of killings discussed; government offers $483,000 reward for information leading to arrest of killers; thousands show up at Cali's cathedral to pay respects; he is mourned by Pope John Paul II, who issues statement lauding him as generous and valiant pastor; photo (M)

2002-03-18.title: World Leaders Rethinking Strategy on Aid to Poor
2002-03-18.abstract: Pres Bush and world leaders from 57 other nations will gather, Monterrey, Mex, to rethink strategy to help speed economic growth in developing countries, which are much poorer than at end of cold war despite fast economic integration of 1990's; Bush administration has promised to increase US foreign aid budget by 15% per year, or $5 billion over three years in first real expansion in more than decade, but administration as well as European leaders and poverty experts are deeply divided about wha

2002-03-18.title: Hopes Rise for a Mideast Truce, Despite Attacks
2002-03-18.abstract: Two terrorist attacks in Israel and heavy gunfire in Bethlehem mark fourth day of shuttle diplomacy by US envoy Gen Anthony C Zinni, who appears to make substantive headway toward brokering cease-fire between Israelis and Palestinians; Zinni holds separate meetings in West Bank and Gaza to work out security arrangements for Israeli withdrawal from all Palestinian-controlled areas; reportedly meets with Yasir Arafat in Ramallah; Israeli forces withdraw from most Palestinian areas invaded in large-scale military operations over past two weeks; remain in and around Bethlehem and on fringes of other areas; gunman opens fire in Kfar Saba killing 18-year-old Isreali high school student and wounding several other people before he is shot dead; suicide bomber sets off explosive near bus stop in northern Jerusalem, wounding woman; map; photo (M)

2002-03-18.title: Arafat's Edge: Violence, And Time, on His Side
2002-03-18.abstract: News analysis; Israel's chief concern in conflict with Palestinians is security, hence Bush administration's proposed cease-fire is not just means but end in itself; Palestinians' chief goal is progress toward state, hence cease-fire looks increasingly like obstacle; basic asymmetry explains why Yasir Arafat has for months demanded political negotiations along with security talks, and why he may be starting to get his way; photos (M)

2002-03-18.title: U.S. Planning New Operations to Root Out Scattered Afghan Holdouts
2002-03-18.abstract: American and Afghan officials warn that pockets of Taliban and Al Qaeda fighters remain in southeastern Afghanistan, along border with Pakistan, and that new military operations are needed to defeat them; American commanders ease away from earlier estimates that recent 11-day battle in mountains could prove decisive engagement of five-month-old war; now feel that as many as 1,000 fighters thought to be surrounded during first encounter in Shah-i-Kot may have slipped away, setting scene for further American-led assaults; Russian defense minister, Sergei B Ivanov, citing bitter experience of Soviet occupation troops in Afghanistan in 1980's, says US must be prepared to spend years flushing out fighters; photo (M)

2002-03-19.title: Pakistan's Leaders as Target
2002-03-19.abstract: Most of the people killed or wounded in a church attack in Pakistan on Sunday were foreigners, but a principal target was Pakistan's government. News Analysis, Page A14.

2002-03-19.title: Kofi Annan's Blunt Words Criticizing Israeli Tactics
2002-03-19.abstract: Text of UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan's letter to Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel condemning Israel for waging what he says seems to be all-out conventional war on Palestinian civilians (M)

2002-03-19.title: 34 New West Bank Settlements Spotted
2002-03-19.abstract: Survey published by Peace Now movement says that 34 new Israeli settlement sites have been built in West Bank since Prime Min Ariel Sharon was elected more than year ago (S)

2002-03-19.title: Exiles Wait for Return Of King to Kandahar
2002-03-19.abstract: Afghanistan's tribal elite, who lived in exile while Soviets, warring mujahedeen and Taliban exerted control over country, converge on Kandahar to await return of exiled King Mohammad Zahir Shah, who left Afghanistan in 1973; scions of noble, mostly Pashtun, families, they are also courtiers to king whose lineages ensure they will be welcomed back as demigods; believe only 87-year-old monarch can unite Afghans, giving country new and peaceful order; photos (M)

2002-03-19.title: Cloud Over Pakistan: Is Musharraf's Life Safe?
2002-03-19.abstract: News analysis; while most of people killed or wounded in grenade attack on Protestant church in Islamabad, Pakistan, were foreigners there is little question that principal target was government of Pres Pervez Musharraf, who has become one of point men in Bush administration's war on terrorism; question arises over whether he can survive, not just politically but even physically; photo (M)

2002-03-20.title: Q&A / Jacques Chirac : What message for the globalization talks?'Liberty, equality, fraternity'
2002-03-20.abstract: Following are excerpts from the interview Monday at the Elysee Palace with President Jacques Chirac. The excerpts were transcribed and translated by Chirac's staff. The interview was conducted by the executive editor of the International Herald Tribune, David Ignatius, and its senior correspondent, John Vinocur.

2002-03-20.title: U.S. to Report North Korea Is Not Meeting A-Pact Terms
2002-03-20.abstract: Bush administration officials say that US will refuse to certify that North Korea is complying with its commitments under 1994 accord under which it was to freeze its nuclear activities in exchange for foreign aid; Pres Bush will nevertheless inform Congress that he has also decided to continue fulfilling America's obligations under accord; position appears to be intended to satisfy conservative critics of agreement, who say North Korea may be continuing to develop nuclear weapons at hidden underground sites, while enabling administration to avoid breach with Japan and Soouth Korea, which strongly support accord (M)

2002-03-20.title: White House Wants Chief of Chemical Arms Group to Resign
2002-03-20.abstract: Bush administration publicly calls for resignation of Jose Bustani as director general of Organization for Prohibition of Chemical Weapons, international organization charged with stopping spread of chemical weapons; says he has mismanaged organization, undertaken ill-considered initiatives and demoralized its technical staff; has sought Bustani's resignation through quiet diplomacy for several weeks to no avail (M)

2002-03-20.title: Adviser for Italian Labor Reform Is Killed
2002-03-20.abstract: Marco Biagi, economist and top adviser to Italy's labor minister, Roberto Maroni, is shot dead by two gunmen outside his home in Bologna, and police believe killing is political assassination; Biagi has been helping center-right government draft proposed changes to Italy's labor law, changes that would make it easier to fire workers; photos (M)

2002-03-20.title: Indonesia Begins Trials of Military in East Timor Abuses
2002-03-20.abstract: Four Indonesian army officers go on trial in Jakarta on charges of failing to prevent militia forces linked to army from massacring 27 people at Roman Catholic church in Suai, East Timor, in Sept 1999; this is first such trial of military defendants, and it shows that despite little interest among public, Indonesia finally feels that it must stand by promise made in 2000 to establish special court to try those accused of responsibility for killing of civilians before and after 1999 vote for East Timor's independence; photo (M)

2002-03-21.title: 8 Die in Blast Outside U.S. Embassy in Peru
2002-03-21.abstract: Car bomb explodes near American Embassy in Lima, Peru, killing at least 8 people and wounding 40, just three days before Pres Bush is due to arrive for talks; no one claims responsibility; State Dept official says no American citizens were hurt in explosion; photos (M)

2002-03-21.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 7, but Envoy Presses On
2002-03-21.abstract: Palestinian suicide bomber tears intercity bus into twisted metal, killing seven Israelis in Umm El-Fahm, Israel; despite attack, Israeli and Palestinian negotiators meet with American mediator Anthony C Zinni to try to hammer out cease-fire; Palestinians are demanding immediate withdrawal of Israeli forces to positions they held before violence broke out in Sept 2000; Israelis demand that their withdrawal occur only after Palestinians crack down on terror and illegal weapons; radical group Islamic Jihad claims responsibility for latest suicide bombing; photo (M)

2002-03-21.title: U.S. MIGHT PURSUE QAEDA AND TALIBAN TO PAKISTAN LAIRS
2002-03-21.abstract: American forces in Afghanistan might cross border into Pakistan to capture or kill Al Qaeda and Taliban fighters thought to have found sanctuary there; Maj Gen Franklin L Hagenbeck, commander of American forces, says such an effort would be 'last resort' carried out with approval of Pakistani leaders; American troops are attacked from several directions just 20 miles from border with Pakistan, near Khost; Hagenbeck says it is not clear whether Americans were caught in fighting between Afghan factions or had come under attack from Taliban or Al Qaeda fighters; one American soldier is injured; photo (M)

2002-03-21.title: World Briefing | Europe: Greece: Immigrants Killed In Minefield
2002-03-21.abstract: Two immigrants believed to be Kurds are killed and one seriously wounded while trying to sneak into Greece through minefield; thousands of economic and political refugees try to enter Europe through Greece (S)

2002-03-21.title: New List of Safe AIDS Drugs, Despite Industry Lobby
2002-03-21.abstract: World Health Orgn releases its first list of manufacturers of safe AIDS drugs, move that could help bring down price of AIDS medicines for poor countries; list includes Cipla Ltd, large Indian producer of generics, and three smaller European ones; decision represents setback for pharmaceutical multinationals who want only patent-holders to decide what discounts to offer on their most expensive and profitable products (M)

2002-03-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Anti-Terror Bill Rejected
2002-03-22.abstract: India's upper house of Parliament rejects anti-terrorism bill favored by ruling Hindu nationalists; opponents fear law, which would let suspects be detained for 30 days without appearing in court, would be used against Muslims (S)

2002-03-22.title: Some tough competition heats up the Arctic Winter Games : Now starring in the knuckle hop
2002-03-22.abstract: So you thought the Winter Games were over. In Nuuk, Greenland, and Iqaluit, Canada, the opening ceremony took place only Sunday.

2002-03-22.title: Euro quandary:It's no small change
2002-03-22.abstract: LONDON:—  They're small, nearly valueless — and a nuisance to millions of Europeans.

2002-03-22.title: Up Close, Too Close, to a Suicide Bombing
2002-03-22.abstract: New York Times correspondent Serge Schemamann describes latest suicide bombing in Jerusalem, which he witnessed first-hand; describes noise of explosion, followed by people screaming and running, some people laying on ground injured; says carnage and debris were everywhere; notes that Israeli reaction to suicide bombings is invariably to erase their traces as quickly as possible, as if to say they will not give in to this; says Israelis are living in fear, and wonders how much more they can take (M)

2002-03-22.title: Fox Hunters In England Win a Reprieve
2002-03-22.abstract: British government shies away from promised ban on fox hunting in face of divided Parliament and anger from country's influential countyside; pledges final action on revised proposal in fall (S)

2002-03-23.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Progress Reported In Talks With U.S.
2002-03-23.abstract: United States and Russia narow differences in two days of talks in Geneva on pact to reduce nuclear arms, but differences remain on how warheads will be counted and whether they will be destroyed or stored when taken out of operation (S)

2002-03-23.title: Paralyzed Woman Has Right To Die, a British Judge Rules
2002-03-23.abstract: British High Court judge, Dame Elizabeth Butler-Sloss, rules that paralzyed woman has right to die and that it is her decision rather than her doctors' whether life-support machine should be shut off; woman, 43-year-old former social worker, appealed to court after doctors at hospital where she is in intensive care say ethics blocks them from following her wishes to end her life; woman has needed ventilator to remain alive since ruptured blood vessel in her neck year ago left her unable to breathe unaided (M)

2002-03-23.title: Bush, in Monterrey, Speaks of Conditional Global Aid
2002-03-23.abstract: Pres Bush calls for 'new compact' for global development by insisting that rich nations give foreign aid to poor nations only if poor nations undertake broad range of political, legal and economic reforms, speech to conference on global aid to developing world, Monterrey, Mexico; photo; 171 nations sign 'Monterrey Consensus,' accord committing them to goals of doubling development to poor and halving world poverty by 2015 (M)

2002-03-23.title: Pakistan Chief Said to Plan A Vote to Extend His Rule
2002-03-23.abstract: Gen Pervez Musharraf, Pakistan leader, plans to stay in power for at least another five years by holding referendum on matter, probably in May; photo of Musharraf with Vice Premier Wu Bangguo of China (M)

2002-03-23.title: U.S. Confident of Suspect's Extradition From Pakistan
2002-03-23.abstract: Bush administration is convinced that Ahmed Omar Sheikh, militant accused of killing Daniel Pearl, will eventually be handed over to United States even if Pakistani government follows through with its plan to try him for crime in Pakistan; photo (M)

2002-03-24.title: Riots Shake Friendships And Faiths In India
2002-03-24.abstract: Riots in India that have laid bare simmering hostilities between Hindu majority and Muslim minority have also left secular Indians fearing for future and shaken friendships across divide; Akbar Divecha, retired Muslim judge in Ahmedabab who was rescued by his Hindu friend, Sanjay Shah, discuss shrinking space for secularism in their country (M)

2002-03-24.title: Ex-Leader of Belarus Challenges a Punishment by Pension
2002-03-24.abstract: Former Belarus Pres Stanislav Shushkevich is suffering inventive punishment at hands of current Pres Aleksandr Lukashenko, political enemy who is refusing to have Shushkevich's pension indexed for inflation; Shushkevich says inflation has left monthly pension worth about $1.80, enough for four loaves of excellent brown bread or bottle of vodka, but not both (M)

2002-03-24.title: U.N. Reviews Plans to Protect Its Offices Around the World
2002-03-24.abstract: Sec Gen Kofi Annan releases report on increasing security at UN offices around world (S)

2002-03-24.title: Central America Waiting To Reap Beneifts of Peace
2002-03-24.abstract: Salvadorans await Pres Bush's visit in hopes that US, which lavished money on country during 12-year civil war, will reward peace and political loyalty with economic aid; photo; Central Americans are coming to realise that region is unlikely to regain importance it held during cold war; former US national security official Robert A Pastor says this is moment, when countries have fragile democracies, that US could have most decisive impact on their long-term security (M)

2002-03-24.title: Under Pressure, Chinese Newspaper Pulls Exposé on a Charity
2002-03-24.abstract: China's most influential liberal newspaper, Southern Weekend, reportedly cancels article on misuse of funds at large government-backed charity, Project Hope, which helps poor children stay in school; government pressure suspected (M)

2002-03-25.title: Tribal Leaders in Pakistan Warn the U.S. to Keep Out
2002-03-25.abstract: Tribal leaders from treacherous mountainous areas along Pakistan's border with Afghanistan warn US military not to carry out threat to enter region in pursuit of Al Qaeda fighters: some regard US as enemy and say their people will sacrifice their lives to keep American soldiers off their land; say no foreigner may enter tribal lands without permission and that warning must be taken seriously; war in Afghanistan is seen dragging on indefinitely if tribal areas, which share porous 450-mile border with Afghanistan, become safe haven for Taliban and Al Qaeda fighters to rest and regroup; maps (M)

2002-03-25.title: U.S. PRODS ISRAEL TO ALLOW ARAFAT TO GO TO SUMMIT
2002-03-25.abstract: Vice Pres Dick Cheney says it is important that Yasir Arafat attend upcoming Arab summit in Beirut, development that would require Prime Minister Ariel Sharon of Israel to lift travel ban; says he has no immediate plans to meet with Arafat because of failure to meet US condition that he take steps to establish cease-fire and stop terrorist attacks against Israel; Sharon weighs Cheney's appeal; says Arafat has given no orders to halt attacks; US officials hope Beirut meeting will endorse Saudi peace plan calling for Israel to withdraw from territory it has occupied since 1967 in return for full normalization of relations with Arab nations; fear meeting could turn into cascade of criticism of Israel and US if Israel does not allow Arafat to attend; Saudi and other Arab leaders also urge Cheney to help Arafat attend Beirut meeting (M)

2002-03-25.title: For Sharon, a Painful Choice
2002-03-25.abstract: News analysis; Vice Pres Dick Cheney has presented Israeli Prime Minister Ariel Sharon with dilemma: let Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat escape his siege and attend summit of Arab leaders in Beirut, or risk having Israel look as if it sabotaged Arab bid for peace in Middle East; has asked Sharon to put aside conditions he set for Arafat to travel and let his old nemesis score at least short-term political victory at his expense (M)

2002-03-25.title: Philippine Clashes May Point to Attempt to Rescue American Hostages From Rebels
2002-03-25.abstract: Philippine troops clash several times in last week with Muslim rebels holding American missionary couple Martin and Graciela Burnham hostage, leading to suggestions that attempt to rescue couple from remote jungle island of Basilan may be imminent; map (M)

2002-03-25.title: Anger and Isolation Roil Israeli Arabs
2002-03-25.abstract: 1.4 million Arabs who live inside Israel as full citizens of state are increasingly isolated as tensions rise between Israelis and Palestinians; many have grown increasingly angry and some have become violent, causing alarm within Israeli government; two sides seem to be growing further apart; several Israeli Arab members of Parliament, reflecting views of constituents, are making statements Jewish colleagues consider treasonous; recent public opinion survey indicates many Israeli Jews view Arabs as dangerous fifth column--an enemy from within; some Israeli-Arab residents of Umm al Fahm comment; map; photo (M)

2002-03-26.title: World Briefing | Americas: Central American Unity
2002-03-26.abstract: Central American leaders sign accord to guarantee investment and allow service companies to operate freely in region; say they have made significant advances toward regional economic integration (S)

2002-03-26.title: César Milstein, 74, Who Won Joint Nobel Prize in Medicine
2002-03-26.abstract: Dr Cesar Milstein, who shared 1984 Nobel Prize in Medicine for revolutionary technique to produce antibodies that latch onto specific proteins, dies at age 74; photo (M)

2002-03-26.title: World Briefing | Africa: Uganda: Crocodiles Kill 43 People
2002-03-26.abstract: Ugandan officials armed with automatic weapons patrol Lake Victoria in search of crocodiles so aggressive they frequently overturn small boats and have eaten some 43 people in last six months (S)

2002-03-26.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: Support For North Koreans
2002-03-26.abstract: Some 100 demonstrators rally outside Chinese Embassy, Seoul, South Korea, protesting roundup of North Koreans who fled across border into China; Dr Norbert Vollertsen, who wages international effort to publicize harsh conditions in North Korea, is detained briefly after scuffle with police (S)

2002-03-26.title: World Briefing | Africa: South Africa: Government Must Offer AIDS Drug
2002-03-26.abstract: South African judge affirms government most distribute AIDS drug nevirapine at public hospitals while it appeals ruling that it make drug available; drug cuts risk of transmission of HIV from pregnant mothers to their unborn babies (S)

2002-03-27.title: World Briefing | World: No Progress On Tobacco Curbs
2002-03-27.abstract: Negotiators meeting in Geneva to draft global treaty curbing tobacco use wrap up fourth round of talks without progress (S)

2002-03-27.title: World Briefing | Europe: Macedonia: Guerrillas Fight Each Other
2002-03-27.abstract: Rival groups of ethnic Albanian guerrillas wage fierce nighttime battle, killing two fighters and wounding five bystanders (S)

2002-03-27.title: Killings of Journalists Rose In 2001 From the Year Before
2002-03-27.abstract: Committee to Protect Journalists reports 37 journalists were slain worldwide as direct result of their work in 2001, up from 24 in 2000; says at least 24 were apparently victims of governments or their sympathizers who objected to reporters' work; says number of journalists in prison jumped to 118 from 81 in 2001 (M)

2002-03-27.title: ARAFAT SAYS FEAR OF EXILE WILL KEEP HIM FROM MEETING
2002-03-27.abstract: Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat says he will not attend Arab League summit meeting in Beirut after Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon says he wants right to bar him from returning to West Bank; decision is setback for Bush administration; casts doubt on Saudi peace plan; efforts of United States envoy Gen Anthony C Zinni to mediate Israeli-Palestinian cease-fire also seems stalled; suspicious vehicle halted by Israeli security forces explodes, killing both men inside, before it can reach apparent target, crowded Jerusalem shopping mall; two members of unarmed international monitoring force are ambushed and shot dead, near Hebron; survivor says uniformed Palestinian opened fire on them; security in Israel is extraordinary in advance of Passover; photo (M)

2002-03-27.title: TOLL IN THOUSANDS POSSIBLE AS QUAKE HITS AFGHAN TOWN
2002-03-27.abstract: Earthquake and its aftershocks raze Nahrin, densely-populated city in remote northeast Afghanistan that had been filling up with refugees returning home, and several villages; kill up to 2,000 people, injure 4,000 and leave up to 10,000 people homeless; Afghan government and aid agencies scurry to send aid to hard-to-reach mountain area; maps; photos (M)

2002-03-28.title: Emotions Swirl at Passover Gatherings
2002-03-28.abstract: Jews across New York region welcome in Passover, but with forbidding chill of anger and frustration over latest suicide bombing in Israel; many of those attending Seders say latest bloodshed in Israel, coming after months of almost daily violence, has cast pall over their gatherings, prompting feelings of doubt and guilt over events in Middle East; photo (M)

2002-03-28.title: Nonstate TV In Russia Wins License To Broadcast
2002-03-28.abstract: Team of Russian journalists win right to continue broadcasting on national television after being shut off airwaves in January in bankruptcy proceedings they say were rigged; government commission votes unanimously to award five-year broadcasting license to Media-Sotsium, nonprofit group that includes former Russian Prime Min Yevgeny M Primakov, business magnate Arkady Volsky and some of country's most critical journalists; supporters are doubtful about how much control journalists wil have over how station will be run (M)

2002-03-28.title: U.N. Official Calls for a Much Larger International Force in Afghanistan
2002-03-28.abstract: Kamel Hossain, United Nations's special representative on human rights in Afghanistan, urges significant expansion of international security force there, warning that failing to do this could torpedo efforts to create democratic institutions in war-devastated country; his recommendation runs counter to view of United States and France, which have expressed opposition to expanding 4,800-member International Security Assistance Force (M)

2002-03-28.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: War Prisoners Lose Compensation Bid
2002-03-28.abstract: Tokyo High Court rejects appeal for government compensation for 150,000 civilians and military personnel held prisoner in Asia during World War II; ruling is latest in string of setbacks for people seeking compensation from Japan for war-related atrocities (S)

2002-03-28.title: Valentino sale reveals sour market for high-end firms : Luxury has gone out of fashion
2002-03-28.abstract: PARIS:—  The Valentino fashion house was sold Wednesday, after six months on the block, at a discount that signals the bursting of a seven-year bubble of rising prices for luxury companies — and the end of a feeding frenzy of acquisitions in the luxury market.

2002-03-29.title: A Nation Challenged: The Vaccine; Officials See No Grounds For a Mass Smallpox Effort
2002-03-29.abstract: Federal health officials, even with more abundant supplies of smallpox vaccine expected by end of year, say they still will not recommend mass vaccination to prepare for possible terrorist attack; but they leave themselves plenty of room to change course, saying repeatedly that issue is still open for public discussion; Health and Human Services Sec Tommy Thompson and Dr Anthony S Fauci, head of National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, say that vaccine has significant risks and likelihood of terrorists' using smallpox is considered low; diagram shows how vaccine is dispensed; photo (M)

2002-03-29.title: Pakistani Group Offers Bleak View of Pakistan's Rights Record
2002-03-29.abstract: Pakistani human rights organization issues report painting bleak picture of respect for human dignity there, particularly for women and children; says women have 'almost no rights' and are bound to follow wishes of male family members, that nearly half country's children are malnourished, that physical and sexual abuse of children is 'rampant' and that young boys are smuggled to Middle East to serve as jockeys in camel races; report expands conventional definition of human rights, looking at social and economic conditions, including health and education; State Dept's annual human rights report had similarly critical assessment of Pakistan, although it took note of fact that Pakistani press is able to publish 'relatively freely' (M)

2002-03-29.title: Model of globalization at risk of losing international clout : At Andersen, success backfires
2002-03-29.abstract: LONDON:—  With armies of accountants in offices from Tokyo to Toronto, Arthur Andersen created the model for the globalization of a fragmented industry. Now it is suffering in part because of its success.

2002-03-29.title: Mideast Turmoil; U.N. and the Middle East: Many Calls for Peace
2002-03-29.abstract: United Nations Security Council has taken up issue of Israel and its relations with neigbors many times through years; examples of how council has called on Israel and its neighbors to settle half century of conflict (M)

2002-03-29.title: A Nation Challenged: The Drug Makers; A New Batch of Old Smallpox Vaccine
2002-03-29.abstract: a new batch of old smallpox vaccine*

2002-03-30.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: U.S. To Close Bases
2002-03-30.abstract: US to shut about half of military bases in South Korea over next decade, but will not reduce number of troops from current 37,000; Gen Thomas A Schwartz explains; photo (S)

2002-03-30.title: In Powell's Words, a Plea to Both Sides
2002-03-30.abstract: Excerpts from Sec Colin Powell's news conference on latest violence in Middle East (S)

2002-03-30.title: Strike by a U.S. Plane, Rather Than Enemy Fire, May Have Killed a G.I.
2002-03-30.abstract: First American soldier killed in assault on Al Qaeda and Taliban forces in eastern Afghanistan this month may have died from fire from US warplane, not enemy mortar blasts as initially thought; Gen Tommy Franks announces investigation into death of Special Forces soldier Stanley L Harriman; also reports US now has more than 7,000 troops in Afghanistan, and releases reports on ten incidents of American and allied troops reportedly killed by coalition fire, and of attacks on possible civilian sites (M)

2002-03-30.title: In the Arabs' Struggle Against Israel, There Are Many Players
2002-03-30.abstract: Article explains range of ideologies and allegiances of Palestinian factions involved in terrorism against Israel, and sources of their financing; four major groups are Al Aksa Martyrs Brigades, Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad and Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine; chart on numbers of people killed and modes of attack, including suicide bombings (M)

2002-03-30.title: Pope Is Forced To Curtail Usual Role In Holy Week
2002-03-30.abstract: Pope John Paul II is forced by ill health to severely curtail participation in Holy Week ceremonies at Vatican (S)

2002-03-31.title: A Top Qaeda Aide May Be Held Captive
2002-03-31.abstract: Reports of capture of top Al Qaeda commander, Abu Zubaydah, noted (S)

2002-03-31.title: U.S. Antiterror Forces May Extend Stay on Philippines Island
2002-03-31.abstract: US troops will likely remain in Philippines longer than six months announced when they were dispatched to train Filipino military in counterterrorism and help rescue two American missionary hostages; heart of training by 160 Special Operations forces on Basilan has yet to begin (S)

2002-03-31.title: From Old Files, a New Story Of U.S. Role in Angolan War
2002-03-31.abstract: US involvement in Angola from 1970's reviewed (M)

2002-03-31.title: U.S. Joins Council Vote Telling Israel To Withdraw
2002-03-31.abstract: US joins other UN Security Council members in resolution calling on Israel to withdraw troops from Ramallah and other Palestinian cities (M)

2002-03-31.title: U.N. Opens Rebuilding Effort, With Limits, in Afghanistan
2002-03-31.abstract: UN Security Council authorizes new mission for Afghanistan that will leave most of responsibility for rebuilding with Afghan government (S)
